steps.text: Input masking replaces sensitive field values with aliases before sending to AI. Example: 'prod-db.internal.net' becomes 'db_1'.
This is your tool's input schema. Each field can be protected by clicking its 'Mask' button, or manually enter a field path below.
Enter the field path using dot notation (e.g., 'database.host', 'connection_string'). This identifies which field to mask.
Choose action: 'mask' creates reversible aliases (recommended), 'strip' removes the field entirely (non-reversible, use for sensitive metadata).
Set an alias prefix like 'db_' to generate meaningful aliases: 'db_1', 'db_2', etc. Make it descriptive!
Click 'Add Rule' to save. Now test in 'Try It Out' below - enter a real value, the AI sees only your alias!
